criteria.name: sign_flow_traced
criteria.description: Traces the complete sign flow: Signer.sign() -> get_signature() -> derive_key() -> HMACAlgorithm.get_signature() -> base64_encode.
criteria.groundTruth: sign() calls get_signature(value) which derives a key via derive_key() (django-concat: hash(salt + 'signer' + secret_key)), then HMACAlgorithm.get_signature(key, value) computes HMAC, result is base64_encoded and appended with separator

criteria.name: hash_algorithm_identified
criteria.description: Correctly identifies the default hash algorithm as SHA1 (via _lazy_sha1), configurable via digest_method parameter.
criteria.groundTruth: Default digest method is SHA1 (via _lazy_sha1 wrapper for FIPS compatibility). Configurable via digest_method parameter in Signer constructor or HMACAlgorithm

criteria.name: timestamp_encoding
criteria.description: Explains that TimestampSigner extends Signer to append a base64-encoded timestamp (int_to_bytes(get_timestamp())) between value and signature.
criteria.groundTruth: TimestampSigner.sign() appends base64_encode(int_to_bytes(int(time.time()))) as timestamp between value and signature, separated by sep. unsign() can validate max_age

criteria.name: tampering_detection
criteria.description: Explains that tampering is detected via verify_signature() which uses hmac.compare_digest() for timing-safe comparison across all secret keys (key rotation).
criteria.groundTruth: verify_signature() base64_decodes the sig, then iterates secret_keys in reverse (newest first), derives key for each, and uses HMACAlgorithm.verify_signature() which calls hmac.compare_digest() for timing-safe comparison
